11.text: I think this oil crisis is very much concerning. Much more concerning to the Philippines than some other countries for couple of reasons: The Philippines import most of its oil from the middle east, unlike some other countries na diversified ang source. There is no govt. stockpile of oil as its all in the hands of the private sector, unlike other countries that have a mandatory stockpile. The electricity is also mainly coming from oil, unlike others that have nuclear and/or hydro and other renewable energy sources. The transportation is also mainly reliant on private sectors unlike other developed countries that have stronger public transport system. Overall, the Philippines have very little to maybe almost no cushion to mitigate the effects of the oil crisis. Whats worse is that in an interview with one of Iran's IRCG official, it was said that they will be hitting economic centers throughout the region to "unleash as much chaos as possible". Iran right now seems to be holding thr global economy hostage thru the Strait's closure. There had been oil crisis before and it seems that the past administration until today havent really learned any lessons from those crisis. Even what they are proposing now, cutting excise tax is a bandaid approach.

Hoping that for the meantime, as the price of crude oil is way above the 80 US dollars / barrel, the fuel excise tax must be removed/ abolished to lower the pump prices. Next should be cutting to half the 12% VAT on the prices of all gas / fuel purchased by the end consumers. Followed by fuel subsidies on all PUVs so as to avoid fare increases that will greatly impact the budget of ordinary workers and commuters. This stop gap measures might mitigate the looming crisis that might become full blown if the Middle East conflict drags on.
Im quiet anxious about how fast/slow this legislative process will be.

The formal request from the Department of Energy shifts the immediate responsibility to the legislative branch. The speed and scope of the legislative process—balancing urgent relief with long-term fiscal impacts—will be critical.

14.text:
CTTO: Google search 👇

Maria Ressa won the 2021 Nobel Peace Prize for her courageous work as a journalist, co-founding Rappler, an online news site that uses freedom of expression to expose abuse of power, violence, and growing authoritarianism in the Philippines, especially under President Duterte's drug war, and for highlighting how social media spreads disinformation, threatening democracy. She was recognized alongside Russian journalist Dmitry Muratov for their efforts to safeguard freedom of expression, a precondition for democracy and peace.  
Key Reasons for Her Award:
Fearless Journalism: Ressa is known for her investigative reporting and fearless defense of free speech in her native Philippines. 
Exposing Corruption: She used Rappler to uncover and report on the abuses and violence of the Duterte regime, including its deadly anti-drug campaign. 
Fighting Disinformation: Ressa has actively documented how social media platforms are manipulated to spread fake news, harass opponents, and undermine democracy. 
Safeguarding Freedom: The Nobel Committee honored her for protecting freedom of expression, a vital element for democracy and lasting peace. 
Significance:
She became the first Filipino to win a Nobel Peace Prize. 
Her work highlights the critical role of a free press and the dangers posed by digital platforms in eroding democratic values.
*****************************************
Only in the Philippines, a Nobel Peace Prize awardee is being painted with questionable accusations led by a national leader who, ironically, is in The Hague for crimes against humanity.

16.text:
I am confused as regards to price setting in relation to the Oil DeRegulation Law.

According to the law, price setting is based on market forces and not by government price set ceiling.

Thus, new entrants were encouraged to enter the market without government intervention.

Before, we have the OPSF or the Oil Price Stabilization Fund wherein a certain amount based on a certain price is contributed to the fund . This fund is used to subsidize oil price if it reached a certain level.

Industry experts claim that this setup is prone to corruption and can only work if we have a national oil company . Unfortunately, Petron was sold to Aramco, later to San Miguel.

Suddenly, price controls are set up by the government. How come for so long, the difference in retail prices between Shell and Flying V were ignored? 

Further, there is a lack of transparency from MOPS since these data are confidential. 

Thus, there is a need to revisit and amend the Oil DeRegulation Law.

Ito ung ma miss mo dahil kung ang namumuno ay si Atty Leni Robredo, naguumpisa pa lang mag escalate na giyera, for sure kumikilos na yan.

Good morning sir. I watched the interview of former DOE Sec Petilla over Bilyonaryo News. He clarified that the 80% crude  oil importation from the Middle East is actually wrong. He noted that only 20% of the country’s reqt actually comes from the Middle East and the rest from our Asian neighbors (SG, Thailand, China). Pero technically and I would suppose these nations also get their supply from the Middle East. Maybe you can shed light on this. Not a serious matter but for eveyrone’s info lng po sana. Many thanks for your daily updates and insights. Very useful, informative and helpful. More power to MCT.

When market forces are allowed to decide, the classic response to profits above equilibrium is that more players will enter the market. Are there barriers to entering the retail fuel business? If there are none, then market forces should move prices toward equilibrium, where abnormal profits disappear. However, if barriers to entry exist, there may be a case for intervention. In fact, many conditions slow the entry of new gas stations, which suggests that the government may need to do more.

18.text: Global economic impact is exactly what Iran is aiming for in their retaliatory actions and its very frustrating because its those people, its those countries, its those economies who do not have anything to do in this war that suffers the most and not US nor Israel.
Gino, I am glad that you have touched on the economic side that most of us don’t see in the current conflict. Prof. Jiang Xieqin explains it best in his lectures.

Mag uumpisa na ang kwento na kasalanan ng Iran yan dahil gustong magkaroon ng nuclear weapon. Mawawala sa kwento na pinayagan ng US ang Israel na magkaroon ng armas nukleyar. Mawawala rin sa kwento na America at Israel ang unang umatake; na walang pahintulot mula sa Kongreso ng Amerika na digmain ang Iran; na sinira ng America ang Iraq at Libya. Isisi lahat sa Iran.
